title: carex umbrosa subsp. pseudosabynensis
intro: Carex umbrosa subsp. pseudosabynensis, also known as Carex pseudosabynensis or False Sabine Sedge, is a subspecies of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is native to certain regions and is often used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving cough and cold symptoms, and aiding in digestion. Carex umbrosa subsp. pseudosabynensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional medicine preparations.
